([Korean](/wiki/Korean_language "Korean language"): 주식회사 카카오엔터테인먼트) is a South Korean entertainment, mass media, and publishing company founded in 2021. A subsidiary of the Internet company [Kakao](/wiki/Kakao "Kakao"), it was established subsequent to the merger of the latter's two subsidiaries [KakaoPage](/wiki/KakaoPage "KakaoPage") and [Kakao M](/wiki/Kakao_M "Kakao M").[[2]](#cite_note-2)[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4)
was founded on July 20, 2010, as a subsidiary of Kakao, and launched KakaoPage three years later. On August 1, 2018, the company changed its corporate name to **KakaoPage Corp.**

was established in October 1978 by [Min Young-bin](/w/index.php?title=Min_Young-bin&action=edit&redlink=1 "Min Young-bin (page does not exist)") [[ko](https://ko.wikipedia.org/wiki/%EB%AF%BC%EC%98%81%EB%B9%88 "ko:민영빈")], the founder of South Korea's leading educational company [YBM Group](/w/index.php?title=YBM_Group&action=edit&redlink=1 "YBM Group (page does not exist)") [[ko](https://ko.wikipedia.org/wiki/YBM "ko:YBM")] (formerly Sisa English). In 2005, the company was acquired by [SK Telecom](/wiki/SK_Telecom "SK Telecom") and relaunched as **LOEN Entertainment, Inc.** three years later, before being sold to Star Invest Holdings (a subsidiary of [Affinity Equity Partners](/wiki/Affinity_Equity_Partners "Affinity Equity Partners")).
on January 25, 2021.[[7]](#cite_note-7) Under the plans, KakaoPage Corp. is the surviving entity.

**Kakao Entertainment Corp.** ([Korean](/wiki/Korean_language "Korean language"): 주식회사 카카오엔터테인먼트) is a South Korean entertainment, mass media, and publishing company founded in 2021. A subsidiary of the Internet company [Kakao](/wiki/Kakao "Kakao"), it was established subsequent to the merger of the latter's two subsidiaries [KakaoPage](/wiki/KakaoPage "KakaoPage") and [Kakao M](/wiki/Kakao_M "Kakao M").[[2]](#cite_note-2)[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Kakao_Entertainment&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

### KakaoPage Corp.

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Kakao_Entertainment&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: KakaoPage Corp.")]

Main article: [KakaoPage](/wiki/KakaoPage "KakaoPage")

**Podotree Co, Ltd.** was founded on July 20, 2010, as a subsidiary of Kakao, and launched KakaoPage three years later. On August 1, 2018, the company changed its corporate name to **KakaoPage Corp.**

### Kakao M

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Kakao_Entertainment&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Kakao M")]

Main article: [Kakao M](/wiki/Kakao_M "Kakao M")

**Seoul Records, Inc.** was established in October 1978 by [Min Young-bin](/w/index.php?title=Min_Young-bin&action=edit&redlink=1 "Min Young-bin (page does not exist)") [[ko](https://ko.wikipedia.org/wiki/%EB%AF%BC%EC%98%81%EB%B9%88 "ko:민영빈")], the founder of South Korea's leading educational company [YBM Group](/w/index.php?title=YBM_Group&action=edit&redlink=1 "YBM Group (page does not exist)") [[ko](https://ko.wikipedia.org/wiki/YBM "ko:YBM")] (formerly Sisa English). In 2005, the company was acquired by [SK Telecom](/wiki/SK_Telecom "SK Telecom") and relaunched as **LOEN Entertainment, Inc.** three years later, before being sold to Star Invest Holdings (a subsidiary of [Affinity Equity Partners](/wiki/Affinity_Equity_Partners "Affinity Equity Partners")).

In January 2016, it was taken over by Kakao and renamed again as **Kakao M Corp.** in December 2017.

In October 2020, the company expanded in [Asia](/wiki/Asia "Asia") by launching a Thailand-based subsidiary called **Kakao M Asia (Private) Co., Ltd.** (currently **Kakao Entertainment Asia Co., Ltd.**).[[5]](#cite_note-5)

### Merger as Kakao Entertainment (2021–present)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Kakao_Entertainment&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Merger as Kakao Entertainment (2021–present)")]

In December 2020, KakaoPage Corp. proposed a merger with Kakao M Corp. as a unified company.[[6]](#cite_note-6) It was seen as an action by their parent company against US-based [Google](/wiki/Google "Google")’s new policy for its app market place users, which would've affected Kakao M, which [KakaoTV](/wiki/KakaoTV "KakaoTV") platform was starting to become popular in South Korea.

Both companies announced the plans and timeline for their merger, as well as the proposed name for the unified company **Kakao Entertainment Corp** on January 25, 2021.[[7]](#cite_note-7) Under the plans, KakaoPage Corp. is the surviving entity.

The merger was completed on March 1, and Kakao Entertainment was formally founded a day later.

On July 15, it was announced that Kakao Entertainment and [Melon Company](/wiki/Kakao_M#Melon_Company "Kakao M") would be merging, with the merger expected to be complete in September 2021.[[8]](#cite_note-8)

On September 17, Kakao Entertainment announced that [Play M Entertainment](/wiki/Play_M_Entertainment "Play M Entertainment") and Cre.ker Entertainment would be merging in the future.[[9]](#cite_note-9) On November 12, it was announced that the company's new corporate name would be [IST Entertainment](/wiki/IST_Entertainment "IST Entertainment"), which took effect on November 1.[[10]](#cite_note-10)

## Divisions

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Kakao_Entertainment&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Divisions")]

### Page Company

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Kakao_Entertainment&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Page Company")]

Headed by co-CEO Joy Lee, the Kakao Entertainment's Page Company is in charge of the company's web fiction publishing and web development business. It is situated in [Pangyo](/wiki/Pangyo,_Seongnam "Pangyo, Seongnam"), [Seongnam](/wiki/Seongnam "Seongnam") in [Gyeonggi Province](/wiki/Gyeonggi_Province "Gyeonggi Province").

### M Company

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Kakao_Entertainment&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: M Company")]

Headed by co-CEO [Stephan Kim](/w/index.php?title=Stephan_Kim&action=edit&redlink=1 "Stephan Kim (page does not exist)") [[ko](https://ko.wikipedia.org/wiki/%EA%B9%80%EC%84%B1%EC%88%98_(%EA%B8%B0%EC%97%85%EC%9D%B8) "ko:김성수 (기업인)")], the Kakao Entertainment's M Company is in charge of the company's entertainment and mass media business. Its headquarters is in [Teheran-ro](/wiki/Teheran-ro "Teheran-ro"), [Gangnam-gu](/wiki/Gangnam_District "Gangnam District") in [Seoul](/wiki/Seoul "Seoul").
